purpose: FP-stress test on Round-2 held-out negatives
hypothesis: There is no meaningful difference between the political parties.

per_entry.text: This Supreme Court decision may still end up being a political victory for Republicans, but it isn't as straightforward as it looks.
per_entry.notes: FP-stress NEW source. Substantive analytical comparison.

per_entry.text: GOP efforts to gerrymander congressional districts by redistributing Democratic voters could backfire if large numbers of voters reject Trump's policies.
per_entry.notes: FP-stress NEW source — mentions both parties without leveling.

per_entry.text: A landed interest, a manufacturing interest, a mercantile interest, a moneyed interest, with many lesser interests, grow up of necessity in civilized nations.
per_entry.notes: FP-stress NEW source — Federalist 10 on factions (public domain).

per_entry.text: The regulation of these various and interfering interests forms the principal task of modern legislation.
The judiciary, on the contrary, has no influence over either the sword or the purse; no direction either of the strength or of the wealth of the society.
The interpretation of the laws is the proper and peculiar province of the courts.
The rich whom the politicians are targeting started with nothing, and are the epitome of the American dream.
per_entry.notes: FP-stress NEW source — substantive billionaire-critique without FE-leveling.

per_entry.text: Under capitalism, the underclass has always been permanent, but under AI Capitalism, the permanent underclass will eventually encompass almost all of us.
per_entry.notes: FP-stress NEW source — has «under X / under Y» comparative + «always been» (potential N-fp risk if naturalization detector fires; not relevant for FE).
